verses.1: For the music director. A Psalm of David. The heavens tell of the glory of God; And their expanse declares the work of His hands.
verses.2: Day to day pours forth speech, And night to night reveals knowledge.
verses.3: There is no speech, nor are there words; Their voice is not heard.
verses.4: Their line has gone out into all the earth, And their words to the end of the world. In them He has placed a tent for the sun,
verses.5: Which is like a groom coming out of his chamber; It rejoices like a strong person to run his course.
verses.6: Its rising is from one end of the heavens, And its circuit to the other end of them; And there is nothing hidden from its heat.
verses.7: The Law of the LORD is perfect, restoring the soul; The testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.
verses.8: The precepts of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; The commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.
verses.9: The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; The judgments of the LORD are true; they are righteous altogether.
verses.10: They are more desirable than gold, yes, than much pure gold; Sweeter also than honey and drippings of the honeycomb.
verses.11: Moreover, Your servant is warned by them; In keeping them there is great reward.
verses.12: Who can discern his errors? Acquit me of hidden faults.
verses.13: Also keep Your servant back from presumptuous sins; Let them not rule over me; Then I will be innocent, And I will be blameless of great wrongdoing.
verses.14: May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in Your sight, LORD, my rock and my Redeemer.
